tests.name: createPipeline returns an object with frequency, timeDomain buffers and poll method
acceptance_criteria: Audio source is connected to an AnalyserNode
Frequency data is readable per frame
Time-domain data is readable per frame
tests.setup: Create a mock AudioContext via createMockAudioContext(), call createAnalyser(ctx), then call createPipeline(result.analyser).
assertions: pipeline.frequency is an instance of Uint8Array
pipeline.timeDomain is an instance of Uint8Array

tests.name: pipeline buffers have length equal to frequencyBinCount
acceptance_criteria: Frequency data is readable per frame
Time-domain data is readable per frame
tests.setup: Create pipeline from analyser with fftSize 2048 (frequencyBinCount = 1024).

tests.name: poll() populates frequency and timeDomain buffers in place
acceptance_criteria: Frequency data is readable per frame
Time-domain data is readable per frame
tests.setup: Create pipeline. Configure mock getByteFrequencyData to fill array with 200 and getByteTimeDomainData to fill with 100. Call poll().
assertions: getByteFrequencyData was called with pipeline.frequency buffer
getByteTimeDomainData was called with pipeline.timeDomain buffer

tests.name: poll() returns the same buffer references on every call (no per-frame allocation)
acceptance_criteria: Frequency data is readable per frame
Time-domain data is readable per frame
tests.setup: Create pipeline. Capture references to frequency and timeDomain buffers. Call poll() twice.
assertions: pipeline.frequency after second poll() is the same reference as before first poll()
pipeline.timeDomain after second poll() is the same reference as before first poll()

tests.name: poll() is callable multiple times simulating per-frame usage
acceptance_criteria: Frequency data is readable per frame
Time-domain data is readable per frame
tests.setup: Create pipeline. Call poll() 60 times in a loop (simulating ~1 second of frames).
assertions: No errors thrown during 60 consecutive poll() calls

tests.name: pipeline works independently of gain/mute state
acceptance_criteria: Mute state does not disable analysis
tests.setup: Create analyser bundle, set gainNode.gain.value to 0 (muted). Create pipeline and call poll().

tests.name: pipeline does not access localStorage, sessionStorage, or cookies
acceptance_criteria: Mute state does not disable analysis
tests.setup: Spy on Storage.prototype.getItem, setItem, and document.cookie setter. Create pipeline and call poll() multiple times.

tests.notes: All tests reuse the existing createMockAudioContext helper. The mock getByteFrequencyData and getByteTimeDomainData already fill arrays in place, matching real Web Audio API behavior.

tests.name: getPipeline() returns an AnalyserPipeline after initAudio()
acceptance_criteria: Audio source is connected to an AnalyserNode
Frequency data is readable per frame
Time-domain data is readable per frame
assertions: pipeline.frequency is an instance of Uint8Array
pipeline.timeDomain is an instance of Uint8Array

tests.name: pipeline.poll() works when player state is suspended (autoplay blocked)
acceptance_criteria: Frequency data is readable per frame
Time-domain data is readable per frame
Mute state does not disable analysis
tests.setup: Call loadAndInit(false) so autoplay is blocked. Get pipeline, call poll().

tests.name: pipeline.poll() works when player is muted
acceptance_criteria: Mute state does not disable analysis
tests.setup: Call loadAndInit(true). Confirm player is muted (default state). Get pipeline and poll().

tests.name: pipeline buffers contain data after poll()
acceptance_criteria: Frequency data is readable per frame
Time-domain data is readable per frame
tests.setup: Configure mockAnalyserNode.getByteFrequencyData to fill with 180 and getByteTimeDomainData to fill with 90. Init player, get pipeline, call poll().

tests.notes: These tests require player.ts to expose getPipeline() on the AudioPlayer interface. The existing setupMocks and loadAndInit helpers are reused. mockAnalyserNode is already available in module scope.

Audio source is connected to an Analyser: analyser.test.ts: createPipeline returns an object with frequency, timeDomain buffers and poll method
player.test.ts: getPipeline() returns an AnalyserPipeline after initAudio()
Frequency data is readable per frame: analyser.test.ts: poll() populates frequency and timeDomain buffers in place
analyser.test.ts: pipeline buffers have length equal to frequencyBinCount
analyser.test.ts: poll() is callable multiple times simulating per-frame usage
player.test.ts: getPipeline() returns an AnalyserPipeline after initAudio()
player.test.ts: pipeline.poll() works when player state is suspended
player.test.ts: pipeline buffers contain data after poll()
Time-domain data is readable per frame: analyser.test.ts: poll() populates frequency and timeDomain buffers in place
analyser.test.ts: pipeline buffers have length equal to frequencyBinCount
analyser.test.ts: poll() is callable multiple times simulating per-frame usage
player.test.ts: getPipeline() returns an AnalyserPipeline after initAudio()
player.test.ts: pipeline.poll() works when player state is suspended
player.test.ts: pipeline buffers contain data after poll()
Mute state does not disable analysis: analyser.test.ts: pipeline works independently of gain/mute state
player.test.ts: pipeline.poll() works when player state is suspended
player.test.ts: pipeline.poll() works when player is muted

src/audio/analyser.ts.interface: export interface AnalyserPipeline { readonly frequency: Uint8Array; readonly timeDomain: Uint8Array; poll(): void; }

src/audio/player.ts.interface_changes: Add getPipeline(): AnalyserPipeline | null to AudioPlayer interface
src/audio/player.ts.implementation: Call createPipeline(analyser) during initAudio() and expose via getPipeline() getter
